description_long: This house features a traditional timber-framed design with white plaster infill and visible wooden beams, evoking a classic European or Tudor style. The lower portion of the structure is clad in rough gray stone, providing a sturdy base and rustic appearance. The steeply pitched gable roof is covered in dark brown wooden shingles, extending over the sides for added weather protection. The front facade includes a central wooden door flanked by two small windows, with a single window above, all framed in wood. The rear of the house is simpler, lacking windows or doors, while the sides each have a single window on the upper level. The overall look is quaint and historic, suitable for a village or old-town urban setting.
description: A timber-framed, two-story house with white plaster walls, a steep gable roof, and a stone base, featuring wooden windows and a central front door.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, two lower windows, and a central upper window with visible timber framing.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the steep wooden roof and a single upper window, with the stone base visible below.
description_view_2: Rear view with plain white plaster wall, timber framing, and no windows or doors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, with a single upper window and prominent roof overhang.
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with stone steps, flanked by small windows and timber framing, no porch or overhang.
